concepts.title: Definition of Electric Potential
concepts.description: Electric potential, also known as electrostatic potential, is the potential difference per unit charge at a given point in an electric field. It's measured in volts (V).

concepts.title: Electric Potential due to a Point Charge
concepts.description: The electric potential at a point due to a point charge is given by the formula: V = k * q / r, where V is the electric potential, k is Coulomb's constant, q is the charge, and r is the distance from the charge.

concepts.title: Sign Convention for Electric Potential
concepts.description: For a point charge, the electric potential is positive outside the charge and negative inside. The potential is zero at infinity. For a system of charges, the electric potential is the algebraic sum of the potentials due to each charge.

concepts.title: Mnemonics for Capacitance Formula
concepts.description: To remember the capacitance formula: C = ε0 * A / d, use the mnemonic 'Capacitance is Easy, Always Divide'. This helps you recall that capacitance is directly proportional to the area and inversely proportional to the distance between the plates.

concepts.title: Confusing Capacitance with Resistance
concepts.description: Don't confuse capacitance with resistance. Capacitance is the ability of a conductor to store electric charge, while resistance is the opposition to the flow of electric current. Make sure to use the correct formula and units for each.

concepts.title: Units of Capacitance
concepts.description: The unit of capacitance is the Farad (F), which is equal to 1 coulomb per volt (C/V). Remember that capacitance is measured in Farads, while electric potential is measured in Volts.

concepts.title: Parallel Plate Capacitor
concepts.description: A parallel plate capacitor consists of two conducting plates separated by a dielectric material. The capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor is given by the formula: C = ε0 * A / d, where C is the capacitance, ε0 is the permittivity of free space, A is the area of the plates, and d is the distance between the plates.

concepts.title: Series and Parallel Combination of Capacitors
concepts.description: When capacitors are connected in series, the reciprocal of the total capacitance is the sum of the reciprocals of the individual capacitances. When capacitors are connected in parallel, the total capacitance is the sum of the individual capacitances.
